WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
prerequisite: Background in mathematics, strong programming skills, fundamental knowledge of data structures and algorithms
course_objectives: This course develops a deep understanding of advanced data structures for efficient problem-solving.
Students will analyze, implement, and apply complex structures like trees, heaps, graphs, and hashing.
course_outcomes: CO1: Analyze algorithms using asymptotic notations and apply the Divide & Conquer technique.
CO2: Implement hashing techniques with collision resolution strategies.
CO3: Construct and manipulate tree-based data structures, including BST, AVL, Red-Black, B-Trees, and Huffman coding.
CO4: Apply graph representations and traversal techniques like BFS, DFS, Connected Components, and Topological Sorting.
CO5: Utilize advanced data structures such as Binomial Heaps, Tries, Skip Lists, and Splay Trees.

units.title: Introduction & Divide and Conquer Technique
topics: Orders of Magnitude: Asymptotic notations (O, Ω, Θ)
Growth of functions: constant, logarithmic, linear, polynomial, exponential
Divide & Conquer: Binary search, Merge sort, Quick sort, Randomized quick sort
problem_solving: Finding starting and ending position in sorted array in O(log n)
Merge Sort with counting recursive calls
Quick Sort with last element as pivot
Maximum subarray using Divide and Conquer

units.title: Hashing
topics: Closed Hashing: Linear Probing, Quadratic probing, Double Hashing
problem_solving: Distinct elements in window of size k

units.title: Trees
topics: Binary Search Trees (BST): insert, delete, search
Balanced Trees: AVL Tree operations (insertion, deletion)
problem_solving: Binary tree with in-order, pre-order, post-order traversals
BST: insert, delete, search with in-order display
Construct binary tree from preorder and inorder

units.title: Graph
topics: Sequential and linked Representations: Adjacency Matrices, Adjacency List, Adjacency Multi list
Graph Traversal: Depth First Search (DFS) and Breadth First Search (BFS)

textbooks: Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, Stein - Introduction to Algorithms (3rd ed.), MIT Press, 2009
Horowitz and Sahani - Fundamentals of Data Structures, Galgotia Publications
Lipschutz - Data Structures, Schaum's Outline Series, Tata McGraw-hill
Reema Thareja - Data Structure Using C, Oxford Higher Education
AK Sharma - Data Structure Using C, Pearson Education India
reference_books: V. Aho, J. E. Hopcroft, J. D. Ullman - Data Structures and Algorithms (1st ed.), Pearson, 1983
Dasgupta, Papadimitrou and Vazirani - Algorithms (3rd ed.), McGraw-Hill Education, 2006
Horowitz, Sahni, Rajasekaran - Computer Algorithms (2nd ed.), Silicon Press, 2007
Kleinberg and Tardos - Algorithm Design (1st ed.), Pearson, 2005
